_meta.scaffoldNote: v0.2.0 (2026-05-08): infrastructure / architecture entries promoted from auto-memory after end-of-day verification — these are now Jarvis ground-truth and locked in the v6.5.0 baseline. Org structure / SOPs / comms protocols still need owner upload.

knowledgeBase.content: Parent: CTI Group Worldwide Services Inc. (CEO: Robert Upchurch). Divisions: GHR (cruise/maritime), J-1 Placements (cultural exchange), J-1 Housing, CTI Marine, UNO Division (uniforms), Baron Promotions, Marine Travel. Detailed reporting lines pending upload from Operations Lead.

Standard recruiting lifecycle: source → screen → present → interview → offer → onboarding. Per-domain variations: cruise (STCW + ENG-1 gates), J-1 (DS-2019 + sponsor gate), hospitality (host approval gate). Full step-by-step pending upload.
Onboarding deliverables per program type. Pending upload from Operations Lead.
Offboarding/repatriation deliverables per program type. Pending upload from Operations Lead.

Zoho stack in use: CRM (operational records), Books (org_id 877439787, USD), Desk (support), Projects, Forms. Data flow: Zoho CRM is system of record for partner/candidate records; Zoho Books for finance; Poseidon V6 dashboard reads from Zoho via MCP for live KPIs.

Robert standing order (2026-04-29): M-F 7am-4pm every 10min; weekends only at 8am, 12pm, 4pm. Do NOT revert to a flat 5-min interval — that was rate-limit risky and caused unnecessary token churn. Skills Library refresh cadence is per-agent (see each domain's _meta.refreshFrequency). System Check button runs all health invariants on demand.

Canonical Poseidon folder: 'C:\Users\ceo\OneDrive - CTI Group Worldwide Services Inc\POSEIDON' (single source of truth). Travel backup mirror (auto-synced daily 5:30pm + on idle): 'I:\POSEIDON'. iPhone/iPad backups (NEVER touch): 'I:\Backups\Apple', 'I:\Backups\Archive_from_C'. Cowork app data (NEVER touch): 'C:\Users\ceo\AppData\Roaming\Claude'. If user mentions 'Poseidon' without a path, default to the canonical OneDrive path.

Standing rule: agents draft, never send. Customer-facing emails always require Robert review. Internal Slack/email: agents may post status updates only after Robert sign-off on the channel + cadence. Escalation paths to be formalized by Operations Lead.

Robert standing rule (2026-04-27, NON-NEGOTIABLE). POSEIDON MASTER (poseidon-dashboard-v6.html) carries cruise/maritime ONLY: cruise contracts, cruise lines, ships, sea-based recruiting, Cruise Ship Candidates. CTI GROUP · J1 SYSTEM DASHBOARD (j1-system-dashboard.html) carries J-1 ONLY: J-1 recruiting, J1 candidates, J1 housing, J1 sponsor contracts (Alliance Abroad/CIEE/Greenheart), J1 hosting companies, airline tickets, J1 Housing Finder, J1 Contract Analysis. Each dashboard has an 'Other' sidebar (page id 'other') as ambiguous-item holding area only. Cross-partition data movement → halt + Robert sign-off.

CTI tenant id: ef421d3f-5736-4cca-a38f-e6a4d8607e7e (single-tenant). Application/client id: aff2df6d-cd54-48f3-bd24-3584fd9ea3de. MSAL authority MUST be tenant-specific ('https://login.microsoftonline.com/<tenant-id>') — single-tenant app rejects /common with AADSTS50194. NEVER use loginPopup or acquireTokenPopup — browsers silently block them. Use loginRedirect with a sessionStorage 'poseidon_resume_*' flag and a '_resumeAfterRedirect' IIFE that auto-navigates back. Scopes consented: openid profile email user.read mail.read mail.readwrite mail.send calendars.read calendars.readwrite tasks.read tasks.readwrite files.read.

Microsoft Graph hard-caps the date range on /me/calendarView at 1825 days. Asking for more returns ErrorInvalidRequest (HTTP 400). Production code MUST stay at or below 1825. Current Poseidon split (Robert 2026-05-08): 365 days back + 1460 forward = 1825 days exactly — biased forward because CEO calendars schedule months ahead. Always handle 4xx/5xx visibly; never silent-break.

knowledgeBase.topic: Eastern Time defaults (events / calendar / tasks / email arrival)
knowledgeBase.content: Robert standing order (2026-05-08): ALL displayed timestamps and Graph fetches resolve to Eastern Time. Constants per dashboard: POSEIDON_TZ='America/New_York' (IANA, for client-side Intl); POSEIDON_TZ_WINDOWS='Eastern Standard Time' (Microsoft Graph 'Prefer: outlook.timezone'). The Windows zone name auto-observes DST, switching between EST (UTC-5) and EDT (UTC-4). Helpers fmtET() / fmtETDate() / fmtETTime() are defined globally; every email/calendar display site uses them. Jarvis save_event always writes Outlook events with timeZone:'America/New_York' regardless of device.

When ANY js/poseidon-modules/*.js file changes: bump MODULES_VERSION constant in poseidon-integration.js (loader appends ?v= to every dynamic module URL), bump CACHE_NAME in service-worker.js (so SW activate evicts old cache), and bump the ?v= on the integration loader's <script> tag in poseidon-dashboard-v6.html (so the LOADER itself loads fresh, not cached). Format: YYYY-MM-DD-tag. Without all three bumps, the user's browser may serve a stale module on next reload and System Check fails with 'tool not registered' errors.

knowledgeBase.topic: Service Worker rules (do NOT cache HTML)
knowledgeBase.content: v3+ service-worker.js rule: HTML and navigation requests bypass SW cache (fetch with cache:'no-cache') so a fresh deploy is picked up immediately. Static assets (svg/css/js/images/json) only are network-first with cache fallback for offline. CACHE_NAME must be bumped on every release (any module change) — activate handler deletes every cache that doesn't match. Past failures came from caching HTML aggressively (stale dashboards persist after deploy) — DO NOT add HTML caching back.

When a PR touches calendar / event detail / modal / form / button — actually load the live URL in Chrome (via chrome MCP), click the button, fill the form, watch the result. Run a sweep of related functions, not only the one you changed. Use BOTH the System Check banner AND an interactive sanity pass. The reason this rule exists: in PR #42 the calendar AM/PM change shipped clean syntax but showEventDetail was already broken (looking for #event-detail-content that never existed) and Robert lost a day to clicking empty modals. DO NOT ship calendar/UI changes without clicking through them on live.

Repo at C:\Projects\Poseidon (moved out of OneDrive 2026-05-07 — git was blocked by OneDrive VFS reparse point 0x9000e01a). push.bat at repo root: cd, clean .git/HEAD.lock + index.lock, git status, git add -A, git commit, git push origin main. Repo: Robert-Upchurch/Poseidon, branch main. Live URLs: V6 https://robert-upchurch.github.io/Poseidon/poseidon-dashboard-v6.html · J1 System https://robert-upchurch.github.io/Poseidon/j1-system-dashboard.html. Pages rebuilds in ~60s. Standing computer-use permission granted by Robert.

knowledgeBase.topic: Edit tool truncation rule on large dashboards
knowledgeBase.content: Never use Edit on poseidon-dashboard-v6.html or j1-system-dashboard.html (each ~7700+ lines, ~500KB). Edit silently truncates near 1000-byte boundaries. Use Python via Claude-Workspace/Code-Projects/ scripts: read full file, do exact-string surgery, write back with sanity check that file still ends with </html>. Same rule applies to any dashboard >5000 lines.

knowledgeBase.topic: let/const NOT on window — mirror cross-file globals
knowledgeBase.content: let/const declarations at the top of an inline <script> in the dashboard do NOT attach to window. External scripts (Jarvis modules, anything in js/poseidon-modules/) cannot reach those names. When you add globals you want cross-file, mirror them: window.X = X. Currently mirrored: msalInstance, o365Account, _emailsCache, ALL_LISTINGS, filteredListings. Add to that list when you add new state.

xAI Realtime has exactly 5 voices (per https://docs.x.ai/developers/model-capabilities/audio/voice): leo (male, authoritative), rex (male, confident), sal (neutral), ara (female, warm), eve (female, energetic — xAI default). If you set a voice name not in this list, xAI silently substitutes the default — no error event. The session.updated handler in poseidon-jarvis-grok.js logs 'Voice confirmed' or 'xAI substituted'. Never invent voice names.

On 'input_audio_buffer.speech_started' from xAI Realtime, the handler does three things in order: (1) sendWs response.cancel — cancels the in-flight LLM response. (2) sendWs output_audio_buffer.clear — clears server-side buffered audio. (3) flushPlayback() — locally .stop() + .disconnect() every tracked AudioBufferSourceNode. All three together = silent immediately. response.cancel alone leaves prebuffered audio playing; output_audio_buffer.clear alone leaves locally-queued PCM playing; flushPlayback alone leaves xAI streaming new audio. Every enqueuePcm() call MUST push the source into state.playbackSources and remove it in src.onended so flushPlayback works.

knowledgeBase.topic: Jarvis web search — Agent Tools API + Tavily fallback
knowledgeBase.content: PRIMARY: xAI Agent Tools API. POST https://api.x.ai/v1/responses with model:'grok-4.3' and tools:[{type:'web_search'}]. Citations parsed from BOTH top-level d.citations[] AND inline output[].content[].annotations[] of type:'url_citation'. (xAI deprecated the prior /v1/responses search_parameters shape on 2026-05-08 with HTTP 410 'Live search is deprecated. Please switch to the Agent Tools API.') FALLBACK: Tavily. POST https://api.tavily.com/search with api_key, query, search_depth, max_results, include_answer:true. Key in localStorage('poseidon_tavily_api_key'); Settings UI panel under Poseidon Enhancements. web_search({provider:'auto'|'grok'|'tavily'}) — auto tries Grok first, falls through to Tavily on 4xx/5xx. STANDING RULE: every single-vendor Jarvis capability gets a fallback in code.

read_document({url}) tool handles PDF, Word (.docx), Excel (.xlsx), PowerPoint (.pptx), text/markdown/csv/json. Lazy-loads parsers (PDF.js, mammoth, SheetJS, JSZip) from CDN on first call. Available on both Poseidon V6 Jarvis and Upchurch Financial Jarvis. Companion rule: every dashboard 'status' indicator that says 'Draft ready' / 'Pending' MUST link to the source file in documents/<package>/. Tag: v6.4.0-document-reader.

Jarvis on any CTI dashboard can read content from every other CTI dashboard, including across origins (GitHub Pages ↔ Cloudflare Pages). Architecture: snapshot publisher (?cti-snapshot=1 URL handler postMessages to parent) + read_remote_dashboard Jarvis tool (hidden iframe + postMessage listener, 5-min cache). Verified working both directions Poseidon V6 ↔ J1 System ↔ Upchurch Financial. Tag: v6.3.0-cross-origin-jarvis.

knowledgeBase.topic: Jarvis Skills Library (meta — this very system)
knowledgeBase.content: Multi-agent reference KB at the 'Jarvis Skills' sidebar entry on Poseidon V6 (page id jarvisskills). 6 agent domains: tax-compliance, recruiting-j1 (REFERENCE-only per partition), cruise-staffing, marketing-bd, operations-process (this domain), ghr-platform. Each domain JSON at data/jarvis-skills/<domain>.json with: _meta + knowledgeBase[] + decisionTrees[] + dataSources[] + escalationRules[]. Index at data/jarvis-skills/index.json + globalEscalationRules. Jarvis tools: list_skill_domains, classify_skill_domain (keyword classifier), query_skill (full-content search), read_skill_domain (everything in one call). Pre-renders into DOM after loadAll so read_page_content works without navigation. Tag: v6.5.0.

Pages rebuilds typically in 30-90s after push. Verify with: gh api repos/Robert-Upchurch/Poseidon/pages/builds | python -c 'import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0][\"status\"], json.load(sys.stdin)[0][\"commit\"][:7])'. Status 'building' → wait. Status 'built' AND commit matches HEAD → live. Browser HTTP cache may still serve old js/ files for ~10min — bump MODULES_VERSION + CACHE_NAME for instant invalidation.
